title: The One With All The Thanksgivings

jokes.text: I couldn't possibly eat another bite. I need something sweet.
jokes.setup: Character claims to be completely full
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts themselves by wanting dessert
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction humor, well-executed but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: I'm completely full, I couldn't eat another bite.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Well, maybe just a little dessert.

jokes.text: Monica, your remote doesn't work. You have to lift it and point.
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of having to 'lift it and point'
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not working. I think the batteries are dead.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Have you tried lifting it and pointing it at the TV?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Lifting it and pointing it? What do you think I've been doing, waving it at the wall?

jokes.text: Very nice. The other day, I was at the bus stop... and this lovely fall breeze came and blew this chick's skirt up.
jokes.setup: Seemingly innocent gratitude about fall weather
jokes.punchline: Reveals the real reason he appreciated the breeze
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure, character-appropriate content

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I love fall. The crisp air, the changing leaves, the way the breeze just cuts right through...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, because it blows away the smell of Joey's apartment.

jokes.text: Which reminds me, I'm also thankful for thongs.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to being thankful for underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical escalation, builds on previous joke

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So you're saying you're grateful for your underwear?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, think about it. Without underwear, that could've been a real problem.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm thankful for underwear every day of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's... actually a fair point.

jokes.text: It's not so much an underpant as a feat of engineering.
jokes.punchline: Elevating underwear to engineering marvel
jokes.explanation: Clever perspective shift, quotable phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A thong? That's just a string with delusions of grandeur.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What do you mean? It's like the engineering marvel of underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's a marvel alright. A marvel of discomfort.

jokes.text: And they play with your mind. Is it there? Is it not there?
jokes.punchline: Philosophical existential crisis about underwear
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation to existential territory

dialogue.line: Philosophical existential crisis about underwear

jokes.text: Are you aware that you're still talking? Is anyone thankful for anything else besides a thong?
jokes.punchline: Straight-man reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man intervention, great economy

jokes.text: I don't know. Am I more thankful for my divorce or my eviction?
jokes.setup: Being asked for something to be thankful for
jokes.punchline: Two terrible life events presented as positive options
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect structure, character-specific content

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: What do you have to be thankful for?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm thankful that I didn't get hit by a bus this year.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's great, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, or that my apartment didn't burn down.

jokes.text: And I didn't think you'd be able to come up with anything.
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acknowledgment of the dark humor
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up, maintains the dark comedy tone

dialogue.line: Deadpan acknowledgment of the dark humor

jokes.text: I'm sorry. This is the worst Thanksgiving ever. I am the king of bad Thanksgivings. You can't swoop in with your bad marriage and take that away from me.
jokes.setup: Someone else having a bad Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Chandler's territorial claim over having the worst holidays
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler character comedy, establishes his ownership of misery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Nobody has a worse Thanksgiving than me. I have a monopoly on bad holidays. It's like my thing.

jokes.text: It's a tradition, like the parade. If the parade decided it was gay and abandoned its family.
jokes.setup: Comparing Chandler's story tradition to the Thanksgiving parade
jokes.punchline: Bizarre personification of the parade with family abandonment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor, unexpected comparison

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Every year, the same story. The parade comes to town, floats get bigger, balloons get weirder, and somehow my family still abandons me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, the parade abandons you?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, the parade is family. It's got issues, it shows up once a year, and it always leaves me disappointed.

jokes.text: Now, Chandler, dear... just because we're getting a divorce... doesn't mean we don't love you. It just means... he would rather sleep with the houseboy than with me.
jokes.punchline: Brutally specific reason for divorce involving affair
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with dark twist

dialogue.speaker: Chandler's Mother
dialogue.line: Sweetie, this has nothing to do with you. Your father and I just grew apart.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler's Father
dialogue.line: Yeah, we just realized we wanted different things out of life.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler's Mother
dialogue.line: Especially after I found him in bed with that cocktail waitress from the Improv.

jokes.text: More turkey, Mr. Chandler?
jokes.punchline: The awkward presence of the affair partner
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment, economical callback

dialogue.line: Reference to the affair revelation. The awkward presence of the affair partner

jokes.text: Every time you tell that story, that guy's accent gets thicker and thicker.
jokes.punchline: Calling out how the story changes with each telling
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on storytelling, clever observation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So there I was, right? And Monica says to me...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, no. Actually, Ross was there. And he said it first.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no. It was Joey. Joey definitely said it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? Every time I tell this story, it's different. I don't even know what actually happened anymore.

jokes.text: So are he and your dad still together? No. After they got their own place, the houseboy got his own houseboy.
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of the houseboy chain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation, recursive houseboy concept

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So let me get this straight—your houseboy had an affair with the maid?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Yes, and the maid had an affair with the gardener.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And the gardener had an affair with...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: The pool boy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Of course he did. What about the pool boy?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: The pool boy had an affair with my wife.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your wife had an affair with the pool boy? That's the most clichéd—

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: And my wife had an affair with the houseboy's mother.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, now we're just making up staff.

jokes.text: Should have heard his accent. You're right, yours is worse. You're the king of bad Thanksgivings.
jokes.punchline: Conceding the 'king of bad Thanksgivings' title
jokes.explanation: Good callback, maintains character dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, fine. You win. You are the king of bad Thanksgivings.

jokes.text: Really? Worse than, 'More turkey, Mr. Chandler?'
jokes.setup: Claim of having a worse Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Callback to the memorable houseboy line
jokes.explanation: Direct callback, reinforces the memorable line

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's nothing. One year my houseboy fell down the stairs and broke his leg.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your houseboy? Monica, we grew up in the same house. You never had a houseboy.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, not after that.

jokes.text: Did the little rich boy have a problem with the butler?
jokes.punchline: Mocking dismissal of his trauma
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic dismissal, plays on class dynamics

jokes.text: Of course, you don't, sweetie. You're brand-new.
jokes.punchline: Condescending explanation that she's a new soul
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific spiritual humor

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, you don't remember any of your past lives?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, I don't think I've had any past lives.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, that's because you're a new soul, Phoebe. You've just started your journey.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that makes sense.

jokes.text: Joey got a turkey stuck on his head? It's not like it sounds. It's exactly like it sounds.
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction confirming the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure, maximum economy

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's not that weird. Lots of people have unconventional relationships.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ross, you're dating your sister's best friend who used to be a man.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, that IS weird.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I know! It's stuck!
jokes.setup: Joey with turkey on his head
jokes.punchline: The visual absurdity and panic
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy visual, high impact moment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How'd it get stuck on my head?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You put it on as a hat!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, it seemed like a good idea at the time!

jokes.text: Help me get it off! Plus, it smells really bad in here.
jokes.punchline: Adding the smell complaint to the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation, adds sensory element to visual gag

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God! The turkey is stuck!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just pull it out!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't! My hand is stuck!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: This is the worst Thanksgiving ever.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And it smells like a diaper!

jokes.text: Of course it smells bad. You have your head up a dead animal's ass.
jokes.punchline: Brutally logical explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan logic, highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What is that smell? It's like... death mixed with old cheese.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's just Monica's cooking from last night.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, seriously, what is it?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, if you want the brutally logical explanation: something died in our apartment, decomposed into a cheese-like substance, and now we're experiencing the beautiful marriage of those two smells.

jokes.text: Get that off now! I can't. It's stuck! It has to feed 20 people. They're not going to eat it off your head!
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of people eating turkey off Joey's head
jokes.explanation: Great Monica panic, absurd visual imagery

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We can't serve the turkey off your head, Joey!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Why not? I'm very comfortable being the center of attention.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because people are going to be eating it!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and...?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And they're going to have their mouths all over your head!

jokes.text: I got it! You pull. I'll spread the legs as wide as I can.
jokes.setup: Trying to solve the turkey problem
jokes.punchline: Innocent statement with sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo humor, works but predictable

jokes.text: Now is not the time.
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction timing, maximum economy

jokes.text: It worked! I scared you! I knew it!
jokes.punchline: Revelation it was intentional to scare Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection, recontextualizes entire sequence

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're telling me you put that turkey on your head on purpose?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I wanted to scare you guys.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, that's not funny. That's just weird.

jokes.text: Remember when Ross tried to say 'butternut squash'... and it came out, 'squatternut bosh'?
jokes.punchline: Ross's mispronunciation of vegetables
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific verbal stumble

jokes.text: Yeah, that's the same.
jokes.setup: Comparison between turkey head and mispronunciation
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic agreement to obviously different levels of embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan sarcasm, excellent economy

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So you're comparing a turkey head to mispronouncing a word?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, when you put it that way, they're basically the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, totally identical levels of humiliation.

jokes.text: For me, anyway. And, of course, the Indians.
jokes.punchline: Adding Native Americans to his personal misery
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor, character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Thanksgiving is just about reliving pain. And now I have to add Native Americans to my personal misery.

jokes.text: Monica, can you just call it sex? It really creeps me out when you call it that.
jokes.punchline: Rachel's discomfort with euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast, relatable reaction

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica. It's not a flower. It's a vagina.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, 'vagina' is so clinical.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So is 'giving your flower.' That makes it sound like you're pollinating someone.

jokes.text: And by the way... while we're at it, a guy's thing is not called his 'tenderness.'
jokes.punchline: Another ridiculous Monica euphemism revealed
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation, reveals Monica's character quirk

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm going to go slip into something more comfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What, a coma?

jokes.text: Believe me.
jokes.punchline: Implied personal experience with the terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical button, implies sexual experience

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know what a penis is.

jokes.text: Might want to rethink the lyrics to 'She Feels Weird Since I've Been Gone.'
jokes.setup: Ross asking if things feel weird since he's been at college
jokes.punchline: Revealing he wrote songs about his sister feeling weird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback structure, creepy song implications

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Does it feel weird?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A little bit, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I wrote some songs about it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Songs about what?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: About my sister feeling weird.

jokes.text: Call them Mom and Dad, you loser.
jokes.setup: Chandler calling parents by first names
jokes.punchline: Harsh correction about family formality
jokes.explanation: Standard family dysfunction humor, economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So I've been calling my parents by their first names now.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know, I thought it would make us closer, you know? More like friends.

dialogue.speaker: Nora
dialogue.line: Chandler, we are NOT your friends. We are your parents.

jokes.text: And Chip promised that this time it'd last at least for an entire song.
jokes.setup: Discussion about having sex with Chip
jokes.punchline: Revealing his previous sexual performance was shorter than a song
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about sexual inadequacy

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It was over before the song ended.

jokes.text: Right, Clifford Alvarez? Listen, Roland Cheng...
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous ethnic names they chose
jokes.explanation: Standard fake ID humor, character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We got fake IDs.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What names did you use?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm Turk Turkleton.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Turk Turkleton? And what's mine?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're Randall Mahmood.

jokes.text: It's not that. I just don't want to be stuck here with your fat sister.
jokes.setup: Chandler not wanting to stay at Ross's house
jokes.punchline: Cruel comment about Monica's weight
jokes.explanation: Sets up major plot point, but cruel humor

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't stay at Ross's house. Last time I was there, Monica sat on me and I couldn't breathe for twenty minutes.

jokes.text: Judy, you did it. She's finally full!
jokes.punchline: Father's cruel celebration of daughter's fullness
jokes.explanation: Contributes to Monica's weight issues backstory

dialogue.line: Monica refusing more food. Father's cruel celebration of daughter's fullness

jokes.text: I can't believe you let George Michael slap you.
jokes.punchline: Focus on the slapping rather than the apology
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection on what's embarrassing

jokes.text: Mine had a dwarf that got broke in half, but, you know, whatever.
jokes.setup: Wanting to tell her Christmas story
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning extremely dark content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe absurdist darkness, delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I have a great Christmas story. So, I was living in this box behind a Duane Reade, and on Christmas Eve, the box got wet, so I had to leave it. And I spent the night in the Port Authority with this guy... Oh! But the good news is, I was pregnant!

jokes.text: I had to. There was never any parking by the psychology building.
jokes.punchline: Completely superficial reason for major life decision
jokes.explanation: Absurd priorities, relatable college experience

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm changing my major again.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Again? Monica, that's like the fifth time this year.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, but this time it's different.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: How is it different?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: The new professor is really hot.

jokes.text: She plays for both teams.
jokes.punchline: Innocent phrase with sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo, works because Ross is oblivious

jokes.text: I want him to be naked, and I want to point at him and laugh.
jokes.punchline: Specific, vindictive revenge fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of revenge desire, character-specific

jokes.text: First of all, if you keep calling it that, no one will take it.
jokes.punchline: Practical advice about euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Good callback, practical sexual advice humor

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's like a flower.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, a flower? Please. It's a flower that's gonna get stepped on in a bar somewhere.

jokes.text: Like this dish towel. This feels so good against my cheek.
jokes.punchline: Making a dish towel seem sexual
jokes.explanation: Absurd seduction lesson, good physical comedy setup

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, watch me. You take the dish towel, and you make it seem... sexual.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How do you make a dish towel sexual?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You just... use your body. Like this.

jokes.text: I love the way this box feels against my cheek.
jokes.punchline: Awkward application with macaroni box
jokes.explanation: Monica's awkward attempt at seduction

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to try that thing you do where you tilt your head and look up through your eyelashes.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, no—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I'm doing it right now. See? Seductive.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You look like you're trying to see if there's a spider on the ceiling.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, maybe if you were a box of macaroni, you'd find it irresistible.

jokes.text: And I put the cool steel... against... my body.
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top knife against body move
jokes.explanation: Good escalation, sets up the toe accident

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Over-the-top knife against body move

jokes.text: They're made of wicker!
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous shoe material explanation
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail, perfectly timed delivery

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could the material of my shoe BE any more penetrable?

jokes.text: Toes on Ice, coming soon to Madison Square Garden.
jokes.punchline: Ice show pun
jokes.explanation: Clever pun in crisis situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So you put your toe on ice and now you're in a show?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's not just any show, it's an ice show!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, that's cool.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I think what he means is, you really need to break the ice with the audience.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, and try not to get cold feet about it.

jokes.text: You brought a carrot. This isn't your toe. This is a small, very cold piece of carrot.
jokes.setup: Expectation of bringing the severed toe
jokes.punchline: Revelation of vegetable mix-up
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure, great misdirection payoff

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: You were supposed to bring the severed toe in a bag of ice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I did bring it in a bag.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: This is a bag of frozen vegetables.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh... oh no.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! There's a toe in my kitchen.
jokes.punchline: Monica's horror at toe in her cooking space
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica reaction, chef's horror

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! Is that a toe? There's a toe in my kitchen!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why is there a toe in my kitchen?!

jokes.text: I'm not falling for that one.
jokes.punchline: Dad's awareness of manipulation tactics
jokes.explanation: Good parent-child dynamic humor

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Dad, can I borrow the Porsche?

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But Dad, I really need it.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: Monica, I know what you're doing. You're using that voice.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What voice?

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: That voice. The one where you pretend to be all sweet and innocent. Well, it won't work this time.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But Daddy...

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: No! And don't use the 'Daddy' thing either. I invented that tactic!

jokes.text: That's why for an entire year people called me Sir Limps-a-lot?
jokes.setup: Learning why he lost his toe
jokes.punchline: Revealing the cruel nickname he endured
jokes.explanation: Great escalation, memorable nickname

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So how did you lose your toe?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, in high school, I was teased mercilessly. The kids called me 'Toeless.' I got so upset that I actually lost my toe in a fit of rage.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's terrible! How did you lose it though?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I kicked it off. Literally. I was so angry, I kicked my own toe off.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Toeless? That's the cruelest nickname I've ever heard.

jokes.text: Sir Limps-a-lot. I came up with that.
jokes.punchline: Joey's proud admission of creating it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment, proud of cruel creativity

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I came up with that nickname!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Of course you did.

jokes.text: 'Sorry' doesn't bring back the piggy that cried all the way home.
jokes.setup: Monica's apology for cutting his toe
jokes.punchline: This Little Piggy nursery rhyme reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nursery rhyme callback, perfectly crafted, highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry about your toe!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? It's okay. This little piggy went to the market, this little piggy stayed home, this little piggy had roast beef, this little piggy had none, and this little piggy... went 'wee wee wee' all the way home. Except my little piggy went 'AHHHHH' all the way to the hospital!

jokes.text: I'm a duck. I go quack-quack. I'm happy all the time.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to cheer up Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous duck impression
jokes.explanation: Desperate attempt at cheering up, absurd but sweet

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Come on, Chandler, let me cheer you up with my best duck impression!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: *makes ridiculous duck sounds and waddles around*

jokes.text: I bet this will work.
jokes.punchline: Implied visual gag (turkey dance)
jokes.explanation: Visual callback to turkey incident, high impact visual

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Come on, Chandler. I'll cheer you up. Watch this!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: (does turkey dance)

jokes.text: Nothing. I said you're so great, and then I just stopped talking.
jokes.punchline: Monica's lie about not saying 'I love you'
jokes.explanation: Standard denial humor, relationship awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What did you say?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I didn't say anything.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, you did. I heard you say 'I love you.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I said 'I love... your shoes.'

jokes.text: You said you loved me. I can't believe this! No, I didn't! Yes, you did! No, I didn't! You love me! No, I don't! Stop it, stop it, stop it!
jokes.punchline: Escalating argument with physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation, physical comedy element

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I said it first!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, you didn't! I clearly said 'I love you' before you did!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, I had a whole speech planned. I was going to—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, so now there's a speech? You're going to out-romance me?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not what I'm saying. I'm just saying—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? Fine. Let's call it a tie.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A tie? Monica, this is the most important moment of our lives and you want a tie?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, what do you want? A medal? A certificate?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I want you to admit that I said it first!

tmdb_overview: Although the Thanksgiving meal is great, Chandler is still moody over his parent's divorce during the holiday when he was a boy and Ross is depressed about his second divorce. The group reminisces about their worst Thanksgivings in order to cheer them up, and Monica's worst memory is revealed.
insight: Flashback structure dilutes laughs: 60 jokes spread across five timelines, 3.59/min.
